qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded recursion in PdfParser.get_value() allows denial-of-service via crafted PDF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a FITS GZIP decompression bomb (unlimited data read during GZIP-compressed FITS image decoding, PR #9521). This finding describes a different vulnerability: broken recursion limit in PdfParser.get_value() during PDF dictionary parsing. Different component (PdfParser.py vs FITS decoder), different root cause (max_nesting=-1 never reaching 0 vs unlimited GZIP decompressed bytes), and different file format (PDF vs FITS). Same library (Pillow) and same broad class (DoS) but different vulnerable code and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a FITS GZIP decompression bomb in Pillow's FITS image decoder (unlimited GZIP-compressed data read, PR #9521, fixed in Pillow 12.2.0). The only qualifying finding (pdfparser_unbounded_recursion_dos) targets a different Pillow component (PdfParser.get_value() for PDF parsing) with a different root cause (broken max_nesting recursion counter). It does not involve FITS image decoding, GZIP decompression, or the code path addressed by PR #9521.
evidence: Advisory specifies FITS GZIP decompression bomb in Pillow FITS decoder (PR #9521, commit 3cb854e8b2bab43f40e342e665f9340d861aa628)
Finding pdfparser_unbounded_recursion_dos targets PdfParser.get_value() in src/PIL/PdfParser.py — a PDF parsing component, not the FITS decoder
Advisory CVE-2026-40192 is CWE-400/CWE-770 (uncontrolled resource consumption) specific to FITS GZIP decompression, not PDF recursion
